{"id":"bd-ejx9","title":"EPIC: Evaluate Slint as egui replacement for rust-daq GUI","description":"Comprehensive comparative evaluation of Slint (https://slint.dev/) vs egui for the rust-daq GUI. The current UI crate (~24k LOC, 18 panels, 24+ widgets) uses egui 0.33 with egui_dock for docking, egui_plot for plotting, and targets both native and WASM (gRPC-web). This epic investigates whether Slint's retained-mode rendering, declarative DSL, and superior embedded story justify a migration, or reveals blockers (WASM maturity, missing docking/plotting, Tokio bridging complexity) that make egui the correct long-term choice. Work is isolated to the eval/slint-gui-comparison worktree at ~/code/rust-daq.eval-slint-gui-comparison. Decision gate: three early-kill tests (WASM, docking, plotting) must pass before full evaluation proceeds.","notes":"Independent technical review (planning agent):
- Prior hypothesis: likely no-go unless Slint proves production-grade WASM on the current rust-daq deployment shape. Native retained-mode wins do not offset failure of a required browser deployment.
- Highest-value first test: a Slint WASM app that talks to the daemon over gRPC-web and survives one unary path plus one long-lived streaming path with cancellation/reconnect/backpressure behavior. A static window or a single ListDevices call is not sufficient.
- Biggest missing plan items were added as tasks: bd-hpxn.1 (target/backend/CI matrix), bd-jcn5.1 (license gate), bd-jcn5.2 (interaction parity gate), bd-ssrd.1 (accessibility validation), bd-ssrd.2 (tooling/ecosystem risk).
- Sequencing: keep E2 first, but treat licensing and interaction parity as early-kill items. E3 is too large as the first post-gate step; prove cross-cutting primitives before committing to eight panel rewrites. Start selected benchmark work as soon as minimal WASM/canvas/plot prototypes exist so late benchmark surprises do not invalidate a large E3 spend.
- Decision framework: use hard blockers plus weighted criteria, not a single additive matrix. Suggested blockers: production WASM viability, gRPC-web streaming/reconnect, docking/layout persistence, interaction parity, and licensing/distribution fit. Weighted criteria after blockers: native perf, WASM perf, plotting quality, accessibility, DX/tooling, ecosystem maturity, and migration effort.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T00:05:27Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-07T00:15:58Z"}

new_value: Gate assessment complete: CONDITIONAL-GO. No hard blockers across 7 gates. Key risks: embedded dynamic panels require internal Slint API (ComponentDefinition::factory() absent from public surface), clipboard needs arboard bridge per use site (~8 locations), runtime gRPC-web browser test deferred. E3 proceeds as primitive-validation slice with 4 explicit go/no-go conditions. Full assessment in crates/ui/src/slint_eval/GATE_ASSESSMENT.md

{"notes":"E2 CONDITIONAL-GO: E3 must be scoped as a PRIMITIVE-VALIDATION SLICE before full panel rewrites. Four go/no-go conditions from gate assessment: (1) Runtime gRPC-web streaming verified in actual browser against maitai daemon (unary + streaming + reconnect) — highest-risk unknown. (2) Floating-window docking prototype (create().show() model) validated with lab users — ComponentDefinition::factory() absent from public API means no embedded single-window docking without forking. (3) arboard clipboard bridge built and assessed for compound boilerplate cost across 8 use sites. (4) License decision made at project governance level (Royalty-free v2.0 + AboutSlint widget accepted, or alternative chosen). If condition 1 or 2 fails, close E3 as NO-GO and document blocker. Prioritize: camera viewer, instrument manager/property grid, experiment designer. Deprioritize: settings panel, notification bar."}

{"id":"bd-8qg3","title":"Expand daq-experiment integration test coverage","notes":"## Problem
The daq-experiment crate has limited integration tests for:
- Complex multi-step plans
- Error recovery scenarios
- Concurrent plan execution
- RunEngine state machine transitions

## Current State
Basic unit tests exist but integration coverage is sparse.

## Tests to Add

### Plan Execution Tests
- [ ] Plan with 10+ sequential steps
- [ ] Plan with conditional branching
- [ ] Plan that creates and executes sub-plans
- [ ] Plan with data dependencies between steps

### Error Recovery Tests
- [ ] Step failure with retry policy
- [ ] Hardware error during scan
- [ ] Plan abort and cleanup
- [ ] Timeout during long-running step

### Concurrency Tests
- [ ] Multiple plans queued for execution
- [ ] Plan cancellation while another is queued
- [ ] Resource contention between plans

### State Machine Tests
- [ ] All valid state transitions
- [ ] Invalid transition rejection
- [ ] State persistence across pause/resume

## Files
- crates/daq-experiment/tests/integration_tests.rs (new)
- crates/daq-experiment/tests/plan_tests.rs (expand)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 80%+ code coverage for RunEngine
- [ ] All error paths tested
- [ ] Concurrent execution tested
- [ ] State machine fully tested
Reparented 2026-02-10 into Phase 3 (Lint/Safety/Guardrails) during second cleanup pass.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T16:58:23Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-03T01:07:15Z"}

{"id":"bd-li4i","title":"Add pixel statistics panel","description":"Comprehensive statistics display for image or ROI.

## Statistics
- Count (number of pixels)
- Min, Max, Mean, Median
- Standard deviation
- Sum, Integrated intensity
- Histogram with adjustable bins
- Percentile values (1%, 5%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 95%, 99%)

## Features
- Global (full image) or ROI-specific
- Update in real-time during acquisition
- Copy statistics to clipboard
- Export to CSV
- Temporal plot of mean intensity over time

## Implementation
- Collapsible panel in ImageViewer side panel
- Efficient calculation (incremental for streaming)
- Handle different bit depths correctly","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-12T18:45:35Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-03T01:08:13Z"}

{"id":"bd-6h1c","title":"Add metadata overlay for live acquisition","description":"Display key acquisition metadata overlaid on the image during live view.

## Overlay Information
- Timestamp (absolute and relative to acquisition start)
- Frame number / total frames
- Exposure time
- Gain/analog settings
- Temperature (camera, sample stage)
- Current position (if motorized stage)
- Custom user-defined fields

## Implementation
- Semi-transparent overlay panel (corner position configurable)
- Toggle visibility with hotkey
- Configurable which fields to show
- Burn-in option for recordings (embed in saved frames)
- Font size/color options for visibility on different backgrounds","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-12T18:45:09Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-03T01:07:13Z"}

{"id":"bd-kovl","title":"Add preferences crate for robust settings persistence","description":"Replace or augment eframe storage with the `preferences` crate for better settings management.

## Benefits over raw eframe storage
- Platform-appropriate storage locations
- Automatic serialization/deserialization
- Typed preferences with defaults
- Migration support for schema changes
- Separate files for different preference groups

## Settings to Persist
- Theme preference (already done, could migrate)
- Window size/position
- Recent connections
- Custom colormaps
- Keyboard shortcut customizations
- Panel layouts
- Per-device settings

## Implementation
- Add `preferences` crate dependency
- Create AppPreferences struct with Serialize/Deserialize
- Migrate existing eframe storage usage
- Add preferences versioning for future migrations

## Reference
- serial-monitor-rust uses this pattern","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-12T18:59:49Z","created_by":"TheFermiSea","updated_at":"2026-03-03T01:08:13Z"}
